variant: should i use minoxidil or finasteride for hair loss? compare them

request.message: should i use minoxidil or finasteride for hair loss? compare them

response_body.response: you need both, not one or the other. they work differently: minoxidil stimulates growth and blood flow, finasteride stops the hormone (DHT) that's shrinking your follicles in the first place. most effective protocols combine both. minoxidil is topical or oral, works faster but needs months of consistency. finasteride requires medical supervision because it affects hormone conversion, and some users report side effects. if you don't respond to finasteride, dutasteride is stronger but also needs a doctor. before you start either one, talk to a doctor. hair loss treatments affect your biology and responses vary.

products.name: Kirkland Signature 5% Minoxidil Solution (6-month)
products.description: Generic 5% minoxidil liquid — same active as Rogaine, lower cost.

options.name: minoxidil
pros: stimulates growth and blood flow to follicles
cons: caution if cardiovascular conditions or scalp irritation
growth stimulator only, doesn't block DHT

options.name: finasteride
pros: blocks DHT conversion (the hormone shrinking follicles)
cons: slower to show results than minoxidil
